chunks.text: I ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF S]NDH, CIRCUIT COURT, LARKANA C.P. No.D-205 of 2016 ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF HON'BLtr JUDGE 1. For orders on office objection 'A'. 2. For Hearing of main case. Mr. Ali Nar,i'az Ghanghro, advocate lor the pe titioner. Mr. Safdar Ali Bhutto, advocate for respondents No.5 to 7 Mr. Naimtullah Bhurgari, State Counsel. The petitioner, who is resident of village Allahando Massan, Nasirabad District Kamber Shahdadkot has maintained this perition seeking directions to official respondents to perform their obligatory duty in accordance with iaw and to ensure that graveyard situated in the village of the petitioner is saved from the illbgal encroachment made by the land grabbers so also to providc adequate protection to the life, liberty, honour of the petitioner and his family members. On being served, the respondents No. 1, 2, 3 & T have Illed their comments wherein the respondent No.3 has categorically stated that no piece of land has been occupied by the land grabbers pertaining to graveyard. Learned counsel for the petitioner states that this petition may be disposed of by directing the official respondents to ensurc rhe protection of aforesaid graveyard from the land grabbing as well as its destruction from water of nearby ponds. Learned State Counsel contcnds that at present there is no danger to the subject graveyard and so far the question of protcction of the petitioner is concerned, in case he approaches to concerned police officials his grievances would be redressed. il DATE OF HEARING 35 05.09.2018. 37 Learned counsel appearing for private respondents No.S, 6 & 7 states that the private respondents are also resident of same village and they are not involved in any act of encroaching upon the land of the graveyard.

Learned counsel for the petitioner states that this petition may be disposed of by directing the official respondents to ensurc rhe protection of aforesaid graveyard from the land grabbing as well as its destruction from water of nearby ponds. Learned State Counsel contcnds that at present there is no danger to the subject graveyard and so far the question of protcction of the petitioner is concerned, in case he approaches to concerned police officials his grievances would be redressed. il DATE OF HEARING 35 05.09.2018. 37 Learned counsel appearing for private respondents No.S, 6 & 7 states that the private respondents are also resident of same village and they are not involved in any act of encroaching upon the land of the graveyard. We, therefore, dispose of this petition by directing the official respondents to ensure the maintenance of the subjcct gravcl,ard. The petition stands disposed of. Tl I J I M.Y.Panhuar/ ** t
